title: Coronilla cannabina
taxonomy: Coronilla cannabina is classified as a flowering plant, specifically within the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Coronilla, which consists of small, herbaceous plants. intro: Coronilla cannabina, also known as smallflower crownwort, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. Coronilla cannabina contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
